danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles integrated into stage design: spiked walls, landmines, burning ropes, slick floors
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat danger; arena itself becomes threat; environmental storytelling of hazardous battlegrounds
Immediate feedback of threat/damage taken; player danger state

safety_signals.visual_form: Normal mode vs Death Match mode menu distinction
safety_signals.meaning: Normal mode presents safer, non-hazardous combat environment; player choice of difficulty/danger level

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue military uniform (Japan/US) → Brown padded jumpsuit (Europe)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military general uniform visual (original); spiky hair reveal (European variant)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Costume redesign for regional legality; Nazi-like aesthetic removed for European release

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character-specific visual identities based on historical personas
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each fighter distinct visual design representing their historical period/archetype

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character roster spans historical periods; visual anachronism in shared arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tournament-based progression system; fighters from different eras engage in single combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clean arena environments; stage-specific designs without integrated hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Traditional one-on-one fighting; no environmental threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arenas filled with traps and obstacles: spiked walls, mines, burning ropes, slick surfaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental hazards affect match conditions; damage possible from arena itself; increased tactical complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Brocken character redesign: military uniform → brown jumpsuit; spiky hair reveal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Same character mechanics, visual identity altered for regional legality (German law Nazi-depiction restrictions)

environmental_storytelling.example: Death Match mode arenas feature contextual traps (spiked walls, landmines, burning ropes, slick floors) that communicate danger and create dynamic combat narratives

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character visual design as historical identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Fighter rosters based on actual historical figures mixed with fictional World Heroes; visual design communicates era/archetype without explicit exposition

environmental_storytelling.technique: Regional visual censorship as political narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Brocken's redesign in European release communicates cultural/legal boundaries; Nazi-adjacent imagery removed, character reimagined as sci-fi soldier archetype
Contrast between Normal (clean, traditional arenas) and Death Match (hazard-filled traps) creates implicit narrative of danger escalation inspired by Japanese deathmatch wrestling culture

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standing ready pose; waiting for engagement
Attack/block/throw animations; movement patterns; special move activation (3-button layout in original, configurable in SNES)
Knockback animation; stagger; recoil feedback indicating threat
Identical combat mechanics to international versions, but visual identity communicates compliance with regional legal/cultural standards
Trigger damage on character contact; create environmental danger that affects match outcome; communicate danger through visual design and interaction

notes: World Heroes communicates world-state primarily through visual character design (historical/fictional archetypes), arena environmental design (normal vs hazard-filled), and regional visual compliance (Brocken censorship). The game lacks explicit faction warfare or political allegiance systems; instead, it presents temporal displacement and historical anachronism as core narrative device. Danger is signaled through arena hazards and knockback feedback. Safety/threat distinction is mode-based (Normal vs Death Match). Regional variants demonstrate how visual manifestation systems respond to political/legal constraints.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - World Heroes SNES)
